titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Ernest Henley Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 8, 2018 at 06:50

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, photographs, scrapbooks, printed material, and ephemera relating to the life and work of the writer William Ernest Henley. Correspondents include Robert Fitzroy Bell, his future wife Anna Nancy Boyle, and Robert Louis Stevenson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings by dates of accession: I. 1946-1980 Acquisitions. II. 1986-1995 Acquisitions. III. 2001-2004 Acquisitions.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, in 2014.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as MS Vault Shelves Henley, Uncat MS Vault File, Uncat MS Vault 450, Uncat MS Vault 619, Uncat MS Vault 642, Uncat MSS 116.

The finding aid for this collection is partially compiled from individual catalog cards that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. Information from the catalog cards was repurposed in the finding aid in accordance with current archival descriptive standards. Some catalog cards, copies of which are filed with the collection, include more detailed descriptions of individual items and/or provenance notes. Descriptions for items not represented by catalog cards were derived from accession records, folder labels, or other available descriptive material.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and purchase, 1946-2004. For more information consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: William Ernest Henley (1849-1903)
bioghist.p: William Ernest Henley was a British poet, editor, and critic whose friends included Rudyard Kipling and Robert Louis Stevenson. His works include A Book of Verses (1888), London Voluntaries (1893), and Hawthorn and Lavender (1899).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: MS Vault Shelves Henley, Uncat MS Vault File

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of G. C. Lilly, 1948.

did.unittitle: AL, draft, signed with initials to Robert Louis Stevenson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by draft of a letter in the hand of Henley and Katharine Stevenson de Mattos to Robert Louis Stevenson, and by draft of a letter, in the hand of Katharine Stevenson de Mattos to Robert Louis Stevenson, both undated.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from C. A. Stonehill on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1980.

did.unittitle: 2 notebooks, authograph manuscript draft of poems, with revisions, by William Ernest Henley, including Henley's two series, In Hospital and Life and Death

acqinfo.p: Purchased on the Library Associates Fund, 1962.

did.unittitle: "Hospital Incidents," manuscript, by William Ernest Henley, in an unidentified hand

acqinfo.p: Purchased from C. A. Stonehill on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1980.

did.unittitle: "Maternal Ancestry," typescript, by Robert Louis Stevenson, with corrections in Henley's hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by a short history of Henley's childhood in an unidentififed hand.

acqinfo.p: Purchased from C. A. Stonehill on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1980.

did.unittitle: 5 notebooks containing autograph manuscript extracts from works of Choderlos Laclos, Lope de Vega, Nicolo Machiavelli and others, as well as miscellaneous notes by Henley
3 notebooks containing manuscript extracts from works by Heinrich Heine, Victor Hugo, and other notes and writings by Henley

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David A. Holmes on the Chauncey Brewster Tinker Prize Fund, 1993.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File

did.unittitle: 5 ALS to A. G. Dew-Smith from Henley
"Bare Ruined Quires," manuscript in an unidentified hand

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. on the Chauncey Brewster Tinker Prize Fund, 1995.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File

did.unittitle: 4 ALS to John W. Gilman from Henley

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bernard Quaritch, Ltd. on the Chauncey Brewster Tinker Prize Fund, 2001.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File

did.unittitle: Song of Speed galley proofs, corrected in an unidentified hand; with two copies of the publication (London: David Nutt, 1903), clipping and menu card

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Hart on the Chauncey Brewster Tinker Prize Fund, 2001.

did.unittitle: Typed copy of letter to W. E. Henley from Robert Louis Stevenson, dated 1894 July 15

2 telegrams to William Ernest Henley and Mrs. Henley from Arthur Morrison and "J.W.G" sending anniversary greetings, and 1 telegram to Mrs. Henley from Gertrude and Vernon Blackburn expressing condolences, presumably on the death of her husband.
Music scores, autograph manuscript, in an unidentified hand, for "The Last Whistle," and "Ave Caesar"
Henley bookplates designed by William Nicholson
Autograph manuscript, in an unidentified hand, of pages of a draft of an unidentified playscript
Printed poems by W. E. Henley
"Matri Dilectissimae," printed poem by W. E. Henley, for a memorial service
Memorial cards for Margaret Mackie Bonle and Margaret Emma Henley
Mrs. W. E. Henley visiting cards
Scrapbook containing press clippings relating to the Glasgow Lyrical Society
Correspondence between Mr. and Mrs. K. G. Richmond and solicitors in Edinburgh and London regarding papers of W. M. Henley

Publisher's flyers for Henley's A Book of Verses , a list of David Nutt's publications, and a printed list of the officers and committe members of the Incorporated Society of Authors, Playwrights and Composers
Xenophon: His Ageslaus with Other Opuscula by H. G. Dakyns, corrected page proofs, bound, with autograph note by Dakyns presenting the book to Henley laid in
Photographs of W. E. Henley and family members and friends, most unidentified
Album of newspaper clippings of review of Henley's book "A Book of Verses"
Photograph and prints of the bust of Heny by Rodin
Pages from disbound scrapbook containing newspaper and magazine clippings regarding the life and death of Lord Byron
Scrapbook containing several newspaper clippings regarding Rudyard Kipling's poem, "The Islanders"

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Michael Silverman on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2001.
